items.title: Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension bug enabled silent WhatsApp data theft
items.description: Adobe patched CVE-2026-48294, a flaw in Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension that could let attackers steal WhatsApp Web chats by luring users to a webpage. Guardio Labs researcher Shaked Biner disclosed HermeticReader, a vulnerability chain in the Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension that allowed any attacker-controlled webpage to silently steal a visitor’s WhatsApp chats, contacts, profile name, […]
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window
items.apex_ai_summary: [MEDIUM] Threat Intel advisory. Risk score 5.0/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-30632 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-23T21:16:42Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-30632", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc

items.title: CVE-2026-63454: An authenticated path traversal vulnerability exists in AOS-CX. Successful exploitation of this vulnerability allows an
items.description: An authenticated path traversal vulnerability exists in AOS-CX. Successful exploitation of this vulnerability allows an attacker to copy arbitrary files to a user readable location from the command line interface of the underlying operating system, which could lead to remote code execution.

items.title: CVE-2026-50759: An issue in exo-explore exo 1.0.69 allows a remote attacker to escalate privileges via the GET /state and DELETE /instan
items.description: An issue in exo-explore exo 1.0.69 allows a remote attacker to escalate privileges via the GET /state and DELETE /instance/{instance_id} endpoints with no authentication.

status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-50759 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: GENERIC
references:

logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:

        EventID:

items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-50759 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-23T21:16:42Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-50759", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-50759 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:4765155; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-23, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Medium confidence — EPSS score 0.462 (elevated exploitation probability)
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window

items.title: CVE-2026-59144: Data::RingBuffer::Shared versions before 0.04 for Perl allow a stack buffer overflow via an unvalidated elem_size in rin
items.description:
Data::RingBuffer::Shared versions before 0.04 for Perl allow a stack buffer overflow via an unvalidated elem_size in ring_read_seq.

The attach-time validator ring_validate_header checks the capacity-overflow and total_size consistency of the header but never caps elem_size against the destination size. ring_read_seq does memcpy(out, ring_slot(h, seq), elem_size) with elem_size read raw from the mmap'd segment, copying into a fixed 8-byte destination scalar. An elem_size larger than 8 bytes writes past the destination.

A local peer that can write the backing file can leave the header valid while setting a large elem_size, so the next read copies a file-controlled length into the fixed 8-byte stack buffer, corrupting adjacent stack frames.
